pattern: Running the construction backwards, the largest element was just written as the whole-array sum, so repeatedly subtracting the rest from the maximum (with a modulo shortcut) must drive every element down to one.
hints: Forward construction is hard to search, but each step overwrote one slot with the current total — so the biggest value is always the most recently written one. Undo from there.
To undo a step, replace the maximum with maximum minus the sum of the other elements; if that drops below one, or the others sum to zero while the max exceeds one, it is impossible.

Use a max-heap. Each round, pop the largest, let rest = total - largest; if rest is the whole remainder reduce via largest mod rest to skip many identical steps, push the reduced value back, and stop when everything is one.

edge_inputs.description: single element equal to one is the trivial start
single element greater than one is impossible
all ones is already the start array
large gap needing the modulo shortcut to avoid timing out

stress.description: forward-simulated reachable targets so the answer is always true
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(2, max(2, min(size, 20)))
    arr = [1] * n
    steps = rng.randint(0, 25)
    for _ in range(steps):
        total = sum(arr)
        i = rng.randrange(n)
        arr[i] = total
    return [arr]

complexity.time: O(n log n + log(max) log n)
